title: Roasted Cauliflower Steak with Beet or Mint Yogurt Sauce
description: A roasted cauliflower steak with melted mozzarella served over either a mildly sweet spicy beet yogurt sauce or a refreshing mint yogurt sauce.
steps: Slice the cauliflower into 1-inch thick steaks, keeping the stem intact.
Brush both sides with olive oil and season with paprika, garlic powder, black pepper, and salt.
Roast at 425°F (220°C) for 20 minutes. Flip and roast for another 8–10 minutes until tender.
Top with mozzarella and basil leaves. Bake or broil for 2–3 minutes until the cheese melts.
Spread your preferred yogurt sauce onto a serving plate and place the cauliflower steak on top. Finish with a light sprinkle of paprika.

variations.title: Spicy Sweet Beet Yogurt Sauce
steps: Blend all ingredients until completely smooth.

variations.title: Mint Yogurt Sauce
steps: Blend the mint, cilantro, garlic, chili, lemon juice, and salt into a smooth paste.
Fold into the Greek yogurt and chill before serving.

notes: For crispier edges, roast the cauliflower on a preheated baking sheet.
Paneer can be added on top before the mozzarella for extra protein.
The beet sauce pairs well with smoked paprika, while the mint sauce complements a squeeze of fresh lemon.
